title: ‘Taylor Swift: The Eras Tour’ Is Here. Is It Worthy of the Hype?
format: Sean and Amanda go era by era through the Taylor Swift concert film with Ringer producer Bobby, and can't agree on whether it's actually a movie or just a very long, very lucrative document of one.

films.title: Taylor Swift: The Eras Tour
films.note: A 169-minute cut of three SoFi Stadium shows, directed by Sam Wrench, moving non-chronologically through all ten of Swift's albums. Amanda liked it fine; Sean, a self-described lapsed fan who spent years publicly mocking the Taylor-as-genius discourse, mostly didn't. Both agree it's pure document rather than movie: no behind-the-scenes footage or narrative, just cameras circling a stadium show that's basically identical night to night. Beat Joker to become the biggest October release ever on a $96 million opening weekend.

segments.heading: Concert films and pop docs, for comparison

films.title: Stop Making Sense
films.note: The recent point of comparison: an intimate, theatrical, Pantages-sized show against Eras Tour's stadium-scale "fortress."

films.title: Truth or Dare
films.note: Named as the behind-the-curtain Madonna doc Eras Tour pointedly isn't. Swift already did that version with Miss Americana.

films.title: Miss Americana
films.note: Swift's Netflix doc, brought up as the making-of they didn't get here. Amanda didn't care for it.

films.title: This Is It
films.note: Raised as the Michael Jackson version of a posthumous, tour-scale concert film.

films.title: Renaissance
films.note: Beyoncé's concert film, out in December, discussed as the likely more substantial counterpart. She has a directing credit here and Swift doesn't.
